steps.title: Convert {name} to erasable-syntax TypeScript
steps.description: Rename {path} to .ts and add types using ONLY erasable syntax (type annotations, interfaces, type-only imports — no enums, no namespaces, no parameter properties) so Node type-stripping runs it directly with zero build step. Preserve behavior exactly: same exports, same CLI flags, same output. While converting, audit for silent catches and uninstrumented error paths — fix in place (structured stderr line with area+severity+hint). Update all importers' specifiers in the same change.
acceptance_criteria: file runs directly under node with type stripping (no transpile step)
tsc --noEmit reports zero errors for this file
behavior parity: the module's gate command output is unchanged
no silent catch blocks remain in the file

steps.title: Record behavior-parity evidence for {name}
steps.description: Run the module gate and the tool's own --selftest/--help surface for {path} pre- and post-conversion; attach the diff-free evidence to the task. Any intentional output change must be listed explicitly with a reason.
acceptance_criteria: gate command exit 0 with output equivalent to pre-conversion run
